texto: quoted Lewis Hyde, whose pamphlet on John Berryman and alcohol he had read in his early months at Granada House: “Irony has only emergency use. Carried over time, it is the voice of the trapped who have come to enjoy the cage.” Then he continued: This is because irony, entertaining as it is, serves an almost exclusively negative function. It’s critical and destructive, a ground-clearing….[I]rony’s singularly unuseful when it comes to constructing anything to replace the hypocrisies it debunks.
explicacao: Citação de D.T. Max sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “quoted Lewis Hyde, whose pamphlet on John Berryman and alcohol he had read in his early months at…” articula reflexao, irony, quoted em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: quoted Lewis Hyde, whose pamphlet on John Berryman and alcohol he had read in his early months at Granada House: “Irony has only emergency use. Carried over time, it is the voice of the trapped who have come to enjoy the cage.” Then he continued: This is because irony, entertaining as it is, serves an almost exclusively negative function. It’s critical and destructive, a ground-clearing….[I]rony’s singularly unuseful when it comes to constructing anything to replace the hypocrisies it debunks.

texto: When David Markson wrote in June to complain about an author's getting an award he though should have been his, Wallace gently warned him away from the pitfall of envy: "Mostly I try to remember how lucky I am to be able to write, and doubly, triply lucky I am that anyone else is willing to read it, to say nothing of publishing it. I'm no pollyanna - this keeping-the-spirits-up shit is hard work, and I don't often do it well. But I try... Life is good
explicacao: Orientação direta de D.T. Max sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “When David Markson wrote in June to complain about an author's getting an award he though should …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, all-time-favorites, david-foster-wallace, útil em momentos de trabalho e david-foster-wallace.

texto: Grammar, he saw, was agreement, community, consensus.
